description_long: This object is a vintage, industrial-style washing machine, commonly found in laundromats or communal laundry areas in urban environments. The front features a large, round glass door with a thick metallic rim, used for loading laundry, and a control panel above it with dials, buttons, and a numeric display, all labeled in what appears to be Japanese. The body is constructed from metal panels with a beige finish, showing signs of wear, grime, and rust, especially around the edges and seams. The sides are plain but display some printed text and minor surface stains, while the back has a metallic access panel with warning labels and visible screws. The top surface is flat and bordered by a raised edge, suitable for placing small items temporarily. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, reflecting heavy-duty use in public or semi-public spaces.
description: A vintage, industrial washing machine with a large front glass door, metal body, and a control panel with dials and numeric display, showing signs of wear and use.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a metallic access panel with warning labels and screws, framed by a brown border and beige body panels.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain beige metal panel with some surface stains and a brown top edge, with minimal features.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the large round glass door, control panel with dials and display, and visible wear around the door and panel edges.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, with printed text and minor stains on the beige metal panel, and the brown top edge visible.
